resolved.title: Silent no-op when handler is not registered for scheduled job
resolved.resolution: Added validateOptions() that throws before saving when handler not registered

resolved.title: Handler errors propagate unhandled from async cron callback
resolved.resolution: Added catch block with onError callback, handler errors no longer propagate

resolved.title: No validation on schedule parameters
resolved.resolution: Added input validation: date validity check, positive finite interval check, non-empty cron expression, handler/name validation

resolved.title: Race condition in runCount increment
resolved.resolution: Changed to atomic SQL: UPDATE run_count = run_count + 1

resolved.title: Handler errors in cron callback propagate unhandled
resolved.resolution: Same as P0 fix - catch block with onError

resolved.title: updateLastRun in finally block can mask original error
resolved.resolution: Wrapped DB update in nested try/catch with onError

resolved.title: Cron constructor can throw, leaving orphaned DB records
resolved.resolution: createJob() wraps scheduleJob in try/catch, removes record on failure

resolved.title: start() loop aborts on first scheduleJob failure
CronService.get() and list() leak mutable CronJobRecord references
Unsafe as cast on mode field from database row
resolved.resolution: Added VALID_MODES runtime validation in mapRow

resolved.title: close() does not use try/finally for resource cleanup
pause() and resume() have zero test coverage
resolved.resolution: Added pause/resume tests (returns false for nonexistent, pauses/resumes scheduled job)

resolved.title: Handler error propagation in callback untested
resolved.resolution: Added error handling tests with onError callback verification

resolved.title: start() filtering logic has no test
resolved.resolution: Added persistence tests: skips disabled jobs, skips expired at jobs on start()

resolved.title: stop() method behavior not directly tested
resolved.resolution: Added stop() test verifying jobs cease firing

resolved.title: at(), every(), cron() share near-identical structure

unchanged.title: Package has no consumer in the monorepo
unchanged.note: Expected for new package - will be wired in future integration task

unchanged.title: No logging in cron-service package
unchanged.note: Deferred to integration task when logging infrastructure is wired

unchanged.title: CronJobRecord schedule is polymorphic string
unchanged.note: Acceptable trade-off: single string works cleanly with SQLite persistence layer

findings.title: Package has no consumer in the monorepo
findings.description: No other package imports @proj-airi/cron-service. Expected for a new package - will be wired in a future integration task.
findings.suggestion: Wire CronService into consumer in future integration checkpoint task.

findings.title: No structured logging in cron-service package
findings.description: Package uses onError callback instead of structured logging. Acceptable for library package; logging can be added at integration layer.
findings.suggestion: Add structured logging when wiring into consuming service.

findings.title: CronJobRecord schedule is polymorphic string
findings.description: Schedule field semantic depends on mode. Acceptable trade-off for SQLite persistence simplicity.
findings.suggestion: Consider discriminated union if type grows more complex in future.

findings.title: CronService hardcodes JobStore dependency
findings.description: Direct instantiation of JobStore. Acceptable for current scope; DI can be added when injeca integration happens.
findings.suggestion: Accept JobStore via constructor injection when integrating with DI container.

findings.title: No use-after-close guard on JobStore
findings.description: Calling methods after close() throws raw better-sqlite3 error. Low risk since CronService.close() manages lifecycle.
findings.suggestion: Add closed flag and guard if needed for safety.

findings.title: CronJobRecord mixes configuration and runtime state
findings.description: Single interface for both config and mutable state. Acceptable for current scope.
findings.suggestion: Split into Config + State if interface grows.

findings.title: Exported classes lack JSDoc
findings.description: CronService, JobStore, and exported types lack JSDoc. Type signatures are reasonably self-documenting.
findings.suggestion: Add JSDoc to public API methods for better DX.

findings.title: save() uses 11 positional SQL parameters
findings.description: Positional placeholders are harder to audit. Named parameters would be clearer.

positive_observations: All P0 issues resolved: handler validation now throws upfront, async errors caught with onError callback
Input validation added at API boundary for all three scheduling methods
Atomic SQL increment eliminates race condition in runCount
Per-job error isolation in start() prevents one bad job from blocking others
createJob() cleans up orphaned DB records on schedule failure
try/finally in close() ensures SQLite connection always released
All CronJobRecord fields marked readonly for immutability
VALID_MODES runtime validation in mapRow prevents invalid mode casts
Test coverage expanded: 34 tests covering pause/resume, stop, persistence filtering, error handling, input validation
Real SQLite with temp directories - zero mocks
ESM-compatible: require() replaced with dynamic import() in persistence tests
